aesthetic: Broken Type — a wood-type mourning broadside over the contested two-column folio Bible
anchor: The 1861 wood-type Union recruiting broadside + the two-column folio Bible (book·chapter·verse rules, red rubrication) + the black-bordered mourning notice + the gilt-stamped green cloth of the 1855 Leaves of Grass.
rationale: The corpus argues the war was a theological event: North and South read the SAME plain-text Bible to opposite conclusions with no court of appeal to decide between them (Noll's 'theological crisis'). So the controlling artifact is the contested scriptural page itself — austere black wood-type and red rubric on cool newsprint, bordered like a mourning notice for 620,000 dead. Against this austerity stands Whitman's counter-scripture: the grass-green of the Leaves binding and the gilt of Jubilee. Deliberately NOT ancestry's warm illuminated atlas — monochrome broadside, cool newsprint, slab wood-type display where ancestry is polychrome heraldry, warm rag paper, old-style serif.

fonts.display: 'Rockwell', 'Rockwell Nova', 'Roboto Slab', 'Bookman Old Style', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', 'Nimbus Mono PS', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', monospace
